mnemonics.title: Insertion Sort Behavior
mnemonics.content: INSERT: In S orted E lements C ounting T owards N ext - Insert each element into its correct position in the growing sorted portion

mnemonics.title: Selection Sort Pattern
mnemonics.content: S ELECT S MALL E lement C and O PTION T ogether - Select smallest, put at start, repeat for remaining

mnemonics.title: Bubble Sort Pattern
mnemonics.content: B UBBLE U P L argest E lement - The largest element bubbles up to its correct position in each pass

acronyms.expansion: Insertion Sort - Sorted Sublist grows incrementally
acronyms.usage: Insertion Sort builds sorted list one element at a time

acronyms.expansion: Selection Sort - Smaller elements are Selected and Swapped
acronyms.usage: Each iteration selects minimum from unsorted portion

acronyms.expansion: Bubble Sort - Bigger elements Sink to Bottom gradually
acronyms.usage: After each pass, largest element settles at the end like a bubble rising

acronyms.expansion: Selection Sort Always Does n-1 Assignments
acronyms.usage: Regardless of input, exactly n-1 swaps happen in Selection Sort

visualTips.visualization: Imagine arranging playing cards in your hand. You pick each new card and insert it into its correct position among the cards you're already holding.
Think of selecting the smallest student to stand in the first row, then second smallest for second position, and so on until all are arranged.
Imagine air bubbles in water - the biggest bubble rises to the top fastest. In each pass, the largest unsorted element moves to its final position at the end.
Draw a 3x3 grid comparing Best/Average/Worst cases. Insertion: Best=n, Avg/Worst=n². Selection: All=n². Bubble: Best=n, Avg/Worst=n².

analogies.analogy: Like organizing books on a shelf by height. You pick each book and slide it to its correct position among already organized books.
Like finding the shortest person in a group and placing them first, then finding the next shortest from remaining, and repeating.
Like sorting marbles by size in a tube - shake gently and watch larger marbles rise to top while smaller ones sink to bottom.
Stable sort is like maintaining original order among equal elements - if two students have same marks, the one who came first in original list stays first.
